title: Freziera monsonensis
taxonomy: Freziera monsonensis is classified within the family Theaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the tea family. This classification suggests that Freziera monsonensis shares characteristics with other members of the Theaceae family. The genus Freziera is a part of the order Ericales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Ericales order, the Theaceae family is one of the 14 main families. intro: Freziera monsonensis, also known as the Indian Freziera or Monsoon tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its potential in treating conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Freziera monsonensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of herbal medicines and tonics.
